attacking_style: Employs long-range attacks on high-mountain stages, willing to sacrifice personal stage victory to facilitate team strategy and consolidate GC positions.
team_tactics: Uses aggressive early moves to destabilize race, creating platform for teammates to capitalize while securing classification objectives through bold positioning.
terrain_preference: High-mountain stages with HC climbs (e.g., Angliru) where sustained long-range efforts can split the field and create tactical opportunities.
vulnerability: Limited data suggests focus on classification defense rather than stage hunting may limit outright winning opportunities when leading.
key_patterns: Long-range attacks used strategically for dual purpose: team support and personal classification gains.
Willing to play domestique role while simultaneously advancing own GC/classification objectives.
Aggressive racing on decisive mountain stages to extend classification margins to insurmountable levels.
form_trajectory: positive: Successfully extended white jersey lead while executing team tactics on decisive Angliru stage
